children.name: PlaceOrder
children.purpose: Execute the full 'place' order workflow: validate items, charge payment, reserve inventory, create order record, send notification.
children.behavior: Receives command and order_data; if command is 'place', performs all steps sequentially; returns success/failure result with rollback on failure.

semantic_inputs.name: order_data
semantic_inputs.description: Order payload with items, payment_method, shipping_address, order_id

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order is created, payment charged, inventory reserved; if failure, no side effects
guarantees: Atomicity: all steps succeed or none

children.name: CancelOrder
children.purpose: Execute the full 'cancel' order workflow: verify order exists and not shipped, refund payment, restore inventory, update order status.
children.behavior: Receives command and order_data; if command is 'cancel', performs all steps sequentially; returns success/failure result with rollback on failure.

in_scope: verifying order existence and not shipped

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order status is cancelled, payment refunded, inventory restored; if failure, no side effects

children.name: TrackOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'track' order workflow: return current order status and estimated delivery time.
children.behavior: Receives command and order_data; if command is 'track', queries order status and estimated delivery; returns result.

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result dict with success, order_id, status, message (including delivery time)

decomposition_rationale: The parent OrderSystem function is a command router that delegates to three distinct workflows: PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, and TrackOrder. Each child handles a complete command workflow atomically, with no sibling calls. The parent parses the command and invokes the appropriate child.
